Title: A bacterial nutrition strategy for plant disease control

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during infection; induced by low sucrose in XVM2 medium; co-transcribed with xanP
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xoc infection; xanthosan-containing medium; sucrose signal in XVM2 medium
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dedicated nutrient for Xanthomonas growth and virulence
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Plant apoplast and bacterial cells after uptake
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates xanthosan uptake into Xoc cells and is required for xanthosan-dependent growth and full virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout and complementation; uptake assays; metabolite detection in medium and cell pellets; plant virulence assays; growth assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XanT is the transporter required for xanthosan uptake into bacterial cells. 2. Loss of XanT blocks xanthosan depletion from medium and abolishes intracellular xanthosan detection. 3. xanT knockout reduces lesion length, bacterial growth in rice, and xanthosan-supported growth in minimal medium.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts together with XanP as an uptake-utilization module; epistatic to avrBs2 in virulence pathway context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes virulence reduction and inability to use xanthosan as nutrient
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for anti-virulence strategies aiming to block pathogen nutrient acquisition

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not upregulated like avrBs2 and xanT/xanP during infection
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dedicated nutrient for Xanthomonas growth and virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No significant contribution to lesion development in tested rice infection assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XanR is a cluster-associated putative ROK regulator. 2. xanR knockout did not significantly alter Xoc lesion length in rice. 3. xanR is absent in some Xanthomonas strains, suggesting it is not a core component of the xanthosan virulence system.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested in the results, but evidence does not support a decisive metabolism role
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Likely low priority target compared with AvrBs2, XanT, and XanP

Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Host cell cytosol; synthesized product released to apoplast
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: DEX-inducible transgenic rice expression produced xanthosan in plant cells
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xoc infection; dexamethasone induction in transgenic rice
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts two UDP-α-D-galactose molecules into the sugar phosphodiester xanthosan with UMP released as side product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step; essential virulence-associated biosynthetic step for xanthosan production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dedicated nutrient for Xanthomonas growth and virulence
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Plant intracellular compartment and apoplastic space of infected leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes xanthosan accumulation in host tissues and enhances Xoc virulence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Catalytic-site mutagenesis; transgenic rice expression; TLC and HILIC-ESI-MS metabolite detection; in vitro enzyme assay with immunoprecipitated protein; virulence assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AvrBs2 is the effector enzyme responsible for xanthosan biosynthesis in infected host cells. 2. It specifically uses UDP-α-D-galactose, not UDP-α-D-glucose, to produce xanthosan and UMP. 3. Catalytic residues including H319 and R544 are required for xanthosan production and bacterial virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AvrBs2 homologs from other Xanthomonas pathogens show conserved xanthosan-producing function; mutant D547A retained reduced activity rather than complete loss
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AvrBs2-defined pathway reveals a targetable virulence metabolism node; informs anti-xanthosan disease-control strategies such as degrading xanthosan in planta
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; HILIC-ESI-MS; NMR; mass spectrometry

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during infection; induced by low sucrose in XVM2 medium together with xanT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in rice to degrade xanthosan during infection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xoc infection; xanthosan-containing medium; sucrose signal in XVM2 medium
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Consecutively hydrolyzes two phosphodiester bonds in xanthosan, producing a ring-opened intermediate and then galactose-1-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for utilization of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key utilization step essential for consuming xanthosan and full virulence
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dedicated nutrient for Xanthomonas growth and virulence
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Host apoplast before bacterial uptake; reduced in XanP-expressing rice
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydrolyzes xanthosan for bacterial nutrition; loss reduces virulence, and ectopic plant expression increases disease resistance by lowering xanthosan
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout and complementation; catalytic mutant complementation; in vitro phosphodiesterase assay with purified protein; TLC/NMR/MS product identification; growth assays; transgenic rice disease assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XanP is a xanthosan-specific phosphodiesterase that converts xanthosan into Gal-1-P via pGal-pGal. 2. Catalytic activity is required for Xoc virulence and xanthosan utilization. 3. Ectopic expression of XanP in rice depletes xanthosan and confers broad resistance to Xoc strains without clear growth penalty.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic motifs HxT/S are essential; does not hydrolyze other tested phosphoesters; plant-expressed XanP supports an anti-xanthosan strategy
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant effects on rice agronomic traits or seed metabolome were detected in XanP-expressing lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for transgenic or engineered anti-xanthosan disease resistance in rice and potentially other crops threatened by AvrBs2-containing Xanthomonas
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-ESI-MS/MS seed metabolomics; TLC; NMR; ESI-FT ICR-MS; HILIC-ESI-MS
